title: Word Search
problem_statement: Given a 2D board and a word, return true if the word exists in the board by consecutive letters adjacent horizontally or vertically. You may not reuse the same cell twice.

examples.explanation: The word can be formed by a path through the board.
Cannot reuse the same cell twice.

intuition: Perform DFS from each cell and backtrack, marking visited cells so the current path does not reuse a position.

approaches.name: DFS with Backtracking
approaches.description: Start search from each board cell. Recursively match the word one letter at a time, marking visited cells and backtracking if needed.

interview_qa.q: Why mark visited cells and restore them?
interview_qa.a: Marking prevents cycles during one search path while restoring allows other starting points to reuse the cell.
The search branches in four directions at each step, so the worst-case complexity includes 4^L possibilities for a word of length L.
